description_long: This is a robust hammer featuring a rectangular, straight wooden handle and a solid, metallic head. The head is elongated with a flat striking face on one end and a tapered, wedge-like peen on the other, typical of a cross-peen hammer. The handle appears slightly weathered, with a natural wood grain visible, while the head is dark, likely steel, with a slightly matte finish and a small, lighter patch on the top. The hammer is utilitarian in design, with no visible ergonomic enhancements or grip textures on the handle. There are no visible brand markings or logos on either the handle or the head. The overall appearance suggests a tool built for durability and function, suitable for use in construction, metalworking, or general urban maintenance.
description: A sturdy hammer with a straight wooden handle and a dark, cross-peen steel head, showing a utilitarian and durable design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the cross-peen head with its flat and wedge ends clearly visible.
description_view_1: Narrow end-on view highlighting the slim profile of the head and the rectangular cross-section of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the flat striking face and wedge peen of the head visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the rectangular shape of the head and the alignment of the handle through the head.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, rectangular in cross-section, smooth with visible wood grain, no ergonomic or grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Cross-peen hammer head, steel, with one flat striking face and one wedge-shaped peen, rectangular in profile.
